name: Masala Poha
preparation: Rinse poha in water and drain. Sprinkle turmeric and salt, mix gently.
Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add curry leaves, green chillies, and peanuts. Saute for a minute.
Add chopped onion and diced potato. Cook until potato is soft, about 5 minutes.
Add the poha and mix gently. Cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes.
Squeeze lemon juice, garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Do not soak poha too long or it becomes mushy.

name: Kanda Poha
preparation: Wash poha under running water and drain well. Add turmeric and salt.
Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add curry leaves, green chillies, and peanuts.
Add sliced onions (kanda) and saute until translucent.
Add the drained poha, sugar, and mix gently.
Cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes. Add lemon juice and coriander, serve.
notes: Adding a pinch of sugar is the traditional Maharashtrian way.

name: Indori Poha
preparation: Rinse poha gently and drain. Add turmeric and salt.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, and green chillies.
Add peanuts and onion, saute until onion softens.
Add poha and sugar. Mix gently and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Squeeze generous lemon juice over the poha.
Serve topped with sev, coriander, and grated coconut.
notes: Indori poha is known for its generous lemon juice and sev topping.

name: Batata Poha
preparation: Rinse poha and drain. Add turmeric and salt.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add diced potatoes (batata) and cook until tender, about 6-7 minutes.
Add onions, peanuts, and green chillies. Saute for 2 minutes.
Add the poha and mix gently. Cook for 3-4 minutes on low heat.
Add lemon juice, garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Dice potatoes small so they cook faster and evenly.

name: Aloo Poha
preparation: Wash poha briefly, drain, and add turmeric and salt.
Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds, curry leaves.
Add cubed potato (aloo) and cook covered until soft.
Add onion, peanuts, and chillies. Saute 2 minutes.
Add poha, mix gently, cook 3-4 minutes on low heat.
Finish with lemon juice and coriander.
notes: Cover the pan while cooking potato for faster results.

name: Peanut Poha
preparation: Rinse poha and drain. Add turmeric and salt.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add extra peanuts and roast for a minute.
Add onion and green chillies, saute until soft.
Add poha, sugar, and mix gently. Cook 3-4 minutes.
Add lemon juice and serve.
notes: Extra peanuts add protein and crunch.

name: Coconut Poha
preparation: Rinse poha and drain. Sprinkle turmeric and salt.
Heat coconut oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add peanuts, onion, and green chillies. Saute until onion is soft.
Add poha and grated coconut. Mix gently.
Cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes.
Add lemon juice and serve.
notes: Use fresh coconut for best flavor. Coconut oil enhances the taste.

name: Mint Poha
preparation: Rinse poha and drain. Add turmeric and salt.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves.
Add peanuts, onion, and green chillies. Saute 2-3 minutes.
Add chopped mint leaves and stir for 30 seconds.
Add poha, mix gently, and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add lemon juice and serve garnished with extra mint.
notes: Fresh mint gives a refreshing twist to regular poha.

name: Tomato Poha
preparation: Rinse poha and drain. Add turmeric and salt.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add peanuts, onion, and green chillies. Saute until onion is soft.
Add chopped tomatoes and red chilli powder. Cook until tomatoes soften.
Add poha and mix gently. Cook for 3-4 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Use ripe tomatoes for a naturally tangy flavor.

name: Green Peas Poha
preparation: Rinse poha and drain. Add turmeric and salt.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add green peas and cook for 3-4 minutes until tender.
Add peanuts, onion, and green chillies. Saute 2 minutes.
Add poha and mix gently. Cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add lemon juice, garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Frozen peas work well; no need to thaw before adding.

name: Rava Upma
preparation: Dry roast semolina in a pan until lightly golden and aromatic. Set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, chana dal, urad dal, and cashews.
Add curry leaves, green chillies, and ginger. Saute briefly.
Add onion and cook until translucent.
Add water and salt, bring to a boil.
Slowly add roasted semolina while stirring continuously to avoid lumps.
Cover and cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes until water is absorbed.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Roasting the rava prevents lumps and adds a nutty flavor.

name: Vegetable Upma
preparation: Dry roast semolina until golden. Set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, chana dal, and curry leaves.
Add green chillies and onion, saute until soft.
Add mixed vegetables and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add water and salt, bring to a boil.
Slowly add semolina while stirring continuously.
Cover and cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Cut vegetables small for even cooking.

name: Tomato Upma
preparation: Dry roast semolina until golden. Set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and saute until translucent.
Add tomato puree, red chilli powder, and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add water and salt, bring to a boil.
Add roasted semolina slowly while stirring.
Cover and cook 3-4 minutes on low heat.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: Tomato adds a tangy twist to regular upma.

name: Bread Upma
preparation: Cut bread slices into small cubes.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add green chillies and onion, saute until soft.
Add vegetables and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add tomato, turmeric, salt and cook until tomato softens.
Add bread cubes, mix gently, and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Use day-old bread for better texture. Do not over-mix.

name: Oats Upma
preparation: Dry roast oats for 2-3 minutes. Set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, chana dal, and curry leaves.
Add onion and green chillies, saute until soft.
Add vegetables and cook 2-3 minutes.
Add water and salt, bring to a boil.
Add roasted oats, mix well.
Cook covered for 2-3 minutes on low heat.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Use thick rolled oats for better texture.

name: Semolina Upma
preparation: Dry roast semolina in ghee until aromatic and golden.
In another pan, heat ghee, add mustard seeds, dals, and cashews.
Add curry leaves, chillies, and onion. Saute until soft.
Add water and salt, bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, slowly add semolina while stirring.
Cover and cook on low for 3-4 minutes.
Add lemon juice, mix well and serve.
notes: Ghee gives a richer flavor than oil.

name: Vermicelli Upma
preparation: Heat oil, add mustard seeds, cashews, and curry leaves.
Add green chillies and onion, saute until soft.
Add vegetables and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add water and salt, bring to a boil.
Add roasted vermicelli and mix well.
Cover and cook on low heat for 4-5 minutes until water is absorbed.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Use pre-roasted vermicelli for convenience.

name: Mixed Dal Upma
preparation: Soak all dals together for 30 minutes. Drain.
Dry roast semolina until golden. Set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add soaked dals and roast for 2 minutes.
Add onion and chillies, cook until onion is soft.
Add water and salt, bring to a boil.
Add semolina slowly while stirring. Cook covered for 5 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Mixed dals add extra protein and texture.

name: Coconut Upma
preparation: Dry roast semolina in coconut oil until golden. Set aside.
Heat coconut oil, add mustard seeds, cashews, and curry leaves.
Add onion and chillies, saute until soft.
Add water and salt, bring to a boil.
Add semolina slowly while stirring.
Add grated coconut, mix well.
Cover and cook on low for 3-4 minutes. Serve.
notes: Fresh coconut is key; coconut oil enhances the flavor.

name: Curry Leaf Upma
preparation: Dry roast semolina until golden. Set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal.
Add a generous amount of curry leaves and let them crackle.
Add green chillies and onion, saute until soft.
Add water and salt, bring to a boil.
Add semolina slowly, stirring continuously.
Cover and cook 3-4 minutes. Finish with ghee and serve.
notes: Use fresh curry leaves for the best aroma.

name: Plain Idli
preparation: Soak rice and urad dal with fenugreek seeds separately for 6 hours.
Grind urad dal to a smooth fluffy batter. Grind rice to a slightly coarse paste.
Mix both batters, add salt, and ferment overnight (8-10 hours).
Grease idli molds and pour batter into each mold.
Steam in an idli steamer for 10-12 minutes.
Remove with a wet spoon and serve with chutney and sambar.
notes: Fermentation is key - batter should double in volume.

name: Rava Idli
preparation: Mix semolina with yogurt and water. Let it rest for 20 minutes.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, chana dal, cashews, and curry leaves.
Add green chillies, saute briefly, and add this tempering to the batter.
Add salt and baking soda just before steaming. Mix well.
Pour into greased idli molds.
Steam for 12-15 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
Serve hot with coconut chutney.
notes: Add baking soda just before steaming for fluffy idlis.

name: Oats Idli
preparation: Powder the oats in a blender. Mix with semolina, yogurt, and water. Rest for 15 minutes.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves. Saute briefly.
Add this tempering to the batter along with grated carrot, chillies, and coriander.
Add salt and baking soda. Mix well.
Pour into greased idli molds and steam for 12-15 minutes.
Serve with green chutney.
notes: Oats make this a high-fiber alternative to regular idli.

name: Mini Idli
preparation: Prepare idli batter by soaking, grinding, and fermenting as for plain idli.
Pour batter into mini idli molds (button idli plates).
Steam for 8-10 minutes.
Heat ghee, add mustard seeds and curry leaves for tempering.
Toss the mini idlis in the tempering.
Serve with sambar or podi.
notes: Mini idlis cook faster and are great for snacking.

name: Masala Idli
preparation: Cut leftover idlis into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and saute until golden.
Add bell pepper and tomato. Cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add red chilli powder and pav bhaji masala.
Add idli pieces, toss gently to coat with masala.
Cook for 2-3 minutes. Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Best way to use leftover idlis. Don't over-stir or they'll crumble.

name: Kanchipuram Idli
preparation: Soak rice and urad dal separately for 6 hours. Grind and ferment overnight.
Add pepper, cumin, ginger, curry leaves, and asafoetida to the fermented batter.
Heat ghee and fry cashews until golden. Add to batter.
Mix well and pour into greased idli molds.
Steam for 12-15 minutes.
Serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar.
notes: The generous pepper makes this a temple-style specialty from Kanchipuram.

name: Stuffed Idli
preparation: Prepare potato filling: heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, onion, and chillies.
Add mashed potato, turmeric, salt. Mix well and set aside.
Pour a thin layer of idli batter in greased molds.
Place a small ball of potato filling in the center.
Top with more batter to cover the filling.
Steam for 12-15 minutes. Serve with chutney.
notes: Don't overfill - keep the stuffing small for even cooking.

name: Ragi Idli
preparation: Soak urad dal and rice with fenugreek seeds for 6 hours.
Grind to a smooth batter.
Add ragi flour to the batter and mix well.
Ferment overnight (8-10 hours).
Pour into greased idli molds and steam for 12-15 minutes.
Serve with chutney and sambar.
notes: Ragi adds calcium and iron. The idlis will be darker in color.

name: Multigrain Idli
preparation: Soak rice and urad dal with fenugreek seeds for 6 hours.
Grind to a smooth batter.
Add ragi, jowar, and bajra flours. Mix thoroughly.
Ferment overnight.
Pour into greased idli molds and steam for 12-15 minutes.
Serve with coconut chutney and sambar.
notes: Multiple grains provide diverse nutrients and a hearty flavor.

name: Button Idli
preparation: Pour fermented batter into mini/button idli molds.
Steam for 8-10 minutes.
Heat ghee, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, and dried red chillies.
Add the steamed button idlis to the pan.
Sprinkle idli podi and toss gently to coat.
Serve hot.
notes: Toss gently with podi to avoid breaking the idlis.

name: Plain Dosa
preparation: Soak rice and urad dal with fenugreek seeds separately for 6 hours.
Grind urad dal to a smooth fluffy batter. Grind rice to a slightly coarse paste.
Mix batters, add salt, and ferment overnight.
Heat a flat tawa/griddle. Pour a ladleful of batter and spread in circles.
Drizzle oil around edges. Cook until the bottom is golden and crisp.
Fold and serve with coconut chutney and sambar.
notes: Spread batter quickly in concentric circles for a thin crispy dosa.

name: Masala Dosa
preparation: Prepare filling: heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, and chillies.
Add onion, cook until soft. Add turmeric and mashed potatoes.
Mix well, add salt, and keep filling warm.
Heat tawa, pour batter and spread thin.
Drizzle oil, cook until golden and crisp on bottom.
Place potato filling in center, fold the dosa over it.
Serve with coconut chutney and sambar.
notes: The tawa must be hot enough - test with a drop of water.

name: Rava Dosa
preparation: Mix semolina, rice flour, maida, salt, cumin, and pepper in a bowl.
Add chopped onion, chillies, and curry leaves.
Add water gradually to make a thin, watery batter. Rest 20 minutes.
Heat a tawa, drizzle oil. Pour batter from edges inward in a circular motion.
Do not spread with the back of a ladle - the batter should be thin enough to spread on its own.
Cook until crisp and golden. Fold and serve with chutney.
notes: Batter should be very thin and watery. No fermentation needed.

name: Onion Dosa
preparation: Mix chopped onions, chillies, and coriander in a bowl.
Heat tawa, pour batter and spread thin.
Sprinkle the onion mixture generously over the dosa.
Press gently with the back of a ladle so onions stick.
Drizzle oil around edges. Cook until bottom is golden and crisp.
Fold and serve with chutney and sambar.
notes: Chop onions finely for even distribution.

name: Set Dosa
preparation: Soak rice, urad dal, poha, and fenugreek together for 6 hours.
Grind to a slightly thick, smooth batter.
Add sugar and salt. Ferment overnight - batter should be thick and fluffy.
Heat a tawa on medium-low heat. Pour batter and do NOT spread too thin.
Cover with a lid and cook for 2-3 minutes. Do not flip.
The dosa should be soft and spongy. Serve in sets of 3 with chutney and sambar.
notes: Set dosa is thick and spongy, not crispy. Cook on low heat.

name: Neer Dosa
preparation: Soak rice for 4-5 hours. Drain.
Grind rice with coconut and water to a very thin, smooth batter.
The batter should be watery like milk. Add salt.
Heat a non-stick pan on medium heat. Grease lightly.
Pour a ladleful of batter and swirl the pan to spread evenly.
Cover and cook for 1-2 minutes. The dosa will be thin and lacy.
Gently peel off and serve with coconut chutney or chicken curry.
notes: Neer means water in Kannada - the batter must be very thin.

name: Ragi Dosa
preparation: Mix all flours in a bowl.
Add cumin seeds, chopped onion, chillies, and curry leaves.
Add water gradually to make a thin pouring batter.
Let it rest for 15-20 minutes.
Heat tawa, pour batter and spread in circles.
Drizzle oil and cook until crisp on bottom.
Serve with coconut chutney.
notes: Ragi dosa doesn't need fermentation - quick to make.

name: Oats Dosa
preparation: Powder oats in a blender. Mix with rice flour and semolina.
Add yogurt, water, and mix to a thin batter.
Add onion, chillies, cumin, curry leaves, and salt. Rest 15 minutes.
Heat tawa, pour batter and spread thin.
Drizzle oil and cook until crisp and golden.
Serve with green chutney.
notes: No fermentation needed. Great for a quick healthy breakfast.

name: Pesarattu Dosa
preparation: Soak green moong dal and rice together for 4-5 hours.
Grind to a smooth batter with ginger and green chillies.
Add salt and mix well. No fermentation needed.
Heat a tawa, pour batter and spread thin.
Sprinkle chopped onion on top.
Drizzle oil and cook until crisp and golden.
Serve with ginger chutney and upma.
notes: Pesarattu is a protein-rich Andhra specialty. No fermentation required.

name: Adai Dosa
preparation: Soak all dals and rice together for 2 hours.
Grind to a coarse batter with red chillies. Do not make it too smooth.
Add cumin, curry leaves, onion, and salt.
Heat tawa, pour batter and spread slightly thick (not thin like regular dosa).
Drizzle oil generously. Cook on medium heat.
Flip and cook on other side until golden.
Serve with avial or jaggery butter.
notes: Adai is coarser and thicker than regular dosa. Rich in protein.

name: Aloo Paratha
preparation: Knead wheat flour with water and salt into a soft dough. Rest for 20 minutes.
Mix mashed potatoes with all spices, chillies, ginger, coriander, and salt.
Divide dough and filling into equal portions.
Roll a dough ball, place filling in center, seal edges, and roll out gently.
Heat a tawa, place paratha, cook until brown spots appear.
Flip, apply ghee, and cook the other side until golden.
Serve hot with yogurt, pickle, and butter.
notes: Use dry potato filling to prevent tearing while rolling.

name: Gobi Paratha
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt into a soft dough. Rest 20 minutes.
Grate cauliflower finely. Squeeze out all excess water.
Mix cauliflower with spices, chillies, ginger, coriander, and salt.
Roll dough, stuff with filling, seal, and roll out gently.
Cook on a hot tawa until brown spots appear on both sides.
Apply ghee on both sides while cooking.
Serve hot with yogurt and pickle.
notes: Squeeze all water from cauliflower to prevent soggy parathas.

name: Paneer Paratha
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt into a soft dough. Rest 20 minutes.
Crumble paneer finely. Mix with onion, chillies, spices, coriander, and salt.
Divide dough and filling into equal portions.
Roll dough, stuff with paneer filling, seal, and roll out gently.
Cook on hot tawa with ghee until golden on both sides.
Serve hot with yogurt and green chutney.
notes: Crumble paneer finely for easier stuffing and rolling.

name: Mooli Paratha
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt into a soft dough. Rest 20 minutes.
Grate radish and squeeze out all water thoroughly.
Mix grated radish with chillies, cumin, ajwain, red chilli powder, coriander, and salt.
Roll dough, stuff with mooli filling, seal, and roll gently.
Cook on hot tawa until golden brown on both sides.
Apply ghee while cooking. Serve with butter and yogurt.
notes: Squeezing all water from radish is essential to prevent soggy parathas.

name: Methi Paratha
preparation: Wash and chop methi leaves finely.
Mix flour, methi leaves, chilli, cumin, turmeric, red chilli powder, yogurt, and salt.
Knead into a soft dough using water as needed. Rest 20 minutes.
Divide into balls, roll each into a flat circle.
Cook on hot tawa until golden brown on both sides.
Apply ghee while cooking. Serve with yogurt and pickle.
notes: Methi leaves are mixed into dough, not stuffed separately.

name: Palak Paratha
preparation: Blanch spinach, cool, and make a smooth puree.
Mix flour, spinach puree, chilli, cumin, ajwain, and salt.
Knead into a soft green dough. Rest for 20 minutes.
Divide into balls and roll out flat circles.
Cook on hot tawa until golden on both sides with ghee.
Serve with yogurt and pickle.
notes: Use spinach puree instead of water for kneading the dough.

name: Mixed Veg Paratha
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt into a soft dough. Rest 20 minutes.
Grate and chop all vegetables. Squeeze out excess moisture.
Mix vegetables with chillies, spices, and salt.
Roll dough, stuff with veggie filling, seal, and roll out gently.
Cook on hot tawa with ghee until golden on both sides.
Serve with yogurt and pickle.
notes: Finely chop and drain vegetables well to prevent soggy parathas.

name: Onion Paratha
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt into soft dough. Rest 20 minutes.
Mix onions with chillies, cumin, red chilli powder, amchur, coriander, and salt.
Roll dough, stuff with onion filling, seal, and roll out.
Cook on hot tawa with ghee until golden on both sides.
Serve with yogurt, pickle, and butter.
notes: Add salt to onions just before stuffing to prevent excess water.

name: Pyaaz Paratha
preparation: Mix flour, chopped onions, chillies, cumin, ajwain, red chilli powder, coriander, and salt.
Knead into dough using minimal water (onions release moisture). Rest 15 minutes.
Divide into balls and roll into flat circles.
Cook on hot tawa until golden on both sides.
Apply ghee while cooking. Serve hot.
notes: Onions are mixed into dough directly in this variation.

name: Lachha Paratha
preparation: Knead flour with water, oil, and salt into a soft dough. Rest 30 minutes.
Divide into balls. Roll each into a thin large circle.
Apply ghee on the surface. Make accordion-style pleats.
Coil the pleated strip into a round disc.
Roll out gently into a paratha.
Cook on hot tawa with ghee until golden and flaky on both sides.
Serve with dal, curry, or yogurt.
notes: The key is the pleating technique that creates flaky layers.

name: Besan Chilla
preparation: Mix besan with water to make a smooth, thin batter (like dosa batter).
Add onion, tomato, chillies, coriander, turmeric, red chilli powder, cumin, and salt.
Heat a non-stick pan, grease lightly with oil.
Pour a ladleful of batter and spread into a thin circle.
Cook on medium heat until bottom is golden, flip and cook other side.
Serve with green chutney and yogurt.
notes: Batter should be thin enough to spread easily. No fermentation needed.

name: Moong Dal Chilla
ingredients: 1/3 cup green moong dal, soaked 4 hours
preparation: Soak moong dal for 4 hours. Drain.
Grind dal with ginger, chillies, and a little water to a smooth batter.
Add asafoetida, chopped onion, coriander, and salt. Mix well.
Heat a non-stick pan, grease with oil.
Pour batter and spread into a thin circle.
Cook until golden on both sides.
Serve with mint chutney.
notes: High-protein breakfast. Batter doesn't need fermentation.

name: Oats Chilla
preparation: Powder oats in a blender. Mix with besan.
Add water to make a smooth batter.
Add onion, tomato, chillies, coriander, turmeric, and salt.
Heat a non-stick pan, grease with oil.
Pour batter and spread thin. Cook until golden on both sides.
Serve with green chutney.
notes: A fiber-rich alternative to regular besan chilla.

name: Paneer Chilla
preparation: Mix besan with water to make a smooth batter.
Add crumbled paneer, onion, chillies, coriander, turmeric, and salt.
Heat non-stick pan, grease with oil.
Pour batter and spread into a circle.
Sprinkle extra paneer on top if desired.
Cook until golden on both sides.
Serve with mint chutney or ketchup.
notes: Paneer adds protein and makes the chilla more filling.

name: Spinach Chilla
preparation: Blanch spinach and make a smooth puree.
Mix besan with spinach puree and a little water to make batter.
Add onion, chillies, cumin, and salt.
Heat non-stick pan, grease with oil.
Pour batter and spread thin.
Cook until golden on both sides.
Serve with yogurt or chutney.
notes: Spinach adds iron and turns the chilla a vibrant green.

name: Tomato Chilla
preparation: Mix besan with tomato puree to make a smooth batter.
Add a little water if too thick.
Add onion, chillies, coriander, red chilli powder, and salt.
Heat non-stick pan, grease with oil.
Pour batter and spread thin. Cook until golden on both sides.
Serve with green chutney.
notes: Tomato puree adds tang and moisture to the batter.

name: Mixed Veg Chilla
preparation: Mix besan with water to make a smooth batter.
Add all chopped vegetables, chillies, coriander, turmeric, and salt.
Heat non-stick pan, grease with oil.
Pour batter and spread into a circle.
Cook on medium heat until golden on both sides.
Serve with green chutney and yogurt.
notes: Chop vegetables very fine for even cooking.

name: Corn Chilla
preparation: Mix besan with water to make a smooth batter.
Add corn kernels, onion, chillies, coriander, turmeric, and salt.
Heat non-stick pan, grease with oil.
Pour batter, spread, and press corn kernels into it.
Cook on medium heat until golden on both sides.
Serve with mint chutney.
notes: Sweet corn adds natural sweetness and crunch.

name: Ragi Chilla
preparation: Mix ragi flour and besan with water to make a smooth batter.
Add onion, carrot, chillies, coriander, cumin, and salt.
Heat non-stick pan, grease with oil.
Pour batter and spread thin.
Cook until golden on both sides.
Serve with coconut chutney.
notes: Ragi is rich in calcium and gives a dark earthy color.

name: Bajra Chilla
preparation: Mix bajra flour with water to make a smooth batter.
Add onion, chillies, coriander, cumin, ajwain, and salt.
Heat a tawa, grease with oil or ghee.
Pour batter and spread into a circle.
Cook on medium heat until golden on both sides.
Serve hot with butter, yogurt, or jaggery.
notes: Bajra chilla is warming and great for winter mornings.

name: Plain Daliya
preparation: Dry roast daliya in ghee for 2-3 minutes until aromatic.
Add water and salt. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes.
Stir occasionally until daliya is soft and water is absorbed.
Serve hot as is, or with milk and sugar.
notes: Roasting daliya prevents a mushy texture.

name: Masala Daliya
preparation: Dry roast daliya for 2-3 minutes. Set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, cumin, and curry leaves.
Add onion and green chillies, saute until soft.
Add tomato and cook until soft. Add turmeric.
Add vegetables and cook 2-3 minutes.
Add water, salt, and roasted daliya. Bring to a boil.
Cover and cook on low for 15-20 minutes until soft.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A healthy and filling savory breakfast option.

name: Sweet Daliya
preparation: Dry roast daliya in ghee for 2-3 minutes.
Fry cashews and raisins in remaining ghee. Set aside.
Add milk and water to the daliya. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cook covered for 15-20 minutes.
Add jaggery/sugar and cardamom. Mix well.
Cook for 2-3 more minutes. Top with cashews and raisins.
Serve warm.
notes: Use jaggery for a healthier sweetener option.

name: Vegetable Daliya
preparation: Dry roast daliya for 2-3 minutes. Set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and chillies, saute until soft.
Add all vegetables and turmeric. Cook 3-4 minutes.
Add water, salt, and roasted daliya.
Cover and cook on low for 15-20 minutes.
Add lemon juice and serve.
notes: Add vegetables of your choice for variety.

name: Namkeen Daliya
preparation: Dry roast daliya in ghee until aromatic. Set aside.
In the same pan, add cumin seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and green chillies, saute until soft.
Add turmeric, water, and salt. Bring to a boil.
Add roasted daliya, cover, and cook on low for 15-20 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Namkeen means savory - a simple, quick version.

name: Milk Daliya
preparation: Dry roast daliya in ghee for 2-3 minutes.
Add milk and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and cook covered for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add sugar and cardamom. Mix well.
Cook 2-3 more minutes. Top with sliced almonds.
Serve warm.
notes: Stir occasionally to prevent sticking to the bottom.

name: Coconut Daliya
preparation: Dry roast daliya in ghee. Set aside.
Fry cashews until golden. Set aside.
Add water and coconut milk to the pan. Bring to a boil.
Add roasted daliya and cook covered for 15-20 minutes.
Add jaggery, grated coconut, and cardamom.
Mix well and cook 2-3 more minutes.
Top with cashews and serve.
notes: Coconut milk gives a creamy richness.

name: Khichdi Daliya
preparation: Wash daliya and moong dal. Soak for 15 minutes.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and cook until soft.
Add tomato and turmeric. Cook until tomato softens.
Add dal, daliya, water, and salt.
Pressure cook for 3 whistles or cook covered for 25 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve with yogurt.
notes: A wholesome one-pot meal. Easy to digest.

name: Tomato Daliya
preparation: Dry roast daliya for 2-3 minutes. Set aside.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and chillies, saute until soft.
Add tomato puree and red chilli powder. Cook 3-4 minutes.
Add water, salt, and roasted daliya.
Cover and cook on low for 15-20 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Tangy and flavorful variation of savory daliya.

name: Methi Daliya
preparation: Wash and chop methi leaves. Dry roast daliya. Set aside.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds.
Add onion and chillies, saute until soft.
Add methi leaves and turmeric. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add water, salt, and roasted daliya.
Cover and cook on low for 15-20 minutes.
Serve hot with yogurt.
notes: Methi adds a subtle bitter flavor that complements daliya.

name: Sabudana Khichdi
preparation: Soak sabudana for 6-8 hours or overnight. Drain completely.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds and curry leaves.
Add potato cubes and cook until golden and crisp.
Add green chillies and crushed peanuts.
Add soaked sabudana and salt. Mix gently.
Cook on low heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add lemon juice, garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Soak sabudana until each pearl is soft and separate, not clumpy.

name: Sabudana Vada
preparation: Drain soaked sabudana well.
Mix with mashed potato, ground peanuts, chillies, cumin, coriander, lemon juice, and salt.
Make small round patties with the mixture.
Heat oil in a pan for shallow frying.
Fry vadas on medium heat until golden and crisp on both sides.
Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with green chutney.
notes: Make sure the mixture is dry enough to hold shape.

name: Sabudana Tikki
preparation: Drain soaked sabudana completely.
Mix with mashed potatoes, crushed peanuts, chillies, cumin, coriander, and salt.
Shape into flat round tikkis.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan.
Cook tikkis on medium heat until golden and crispy on both sides.
Serve with tamarind chutney or yogurt.
notes: Tikkis are flatter than vadas and pan-fried, not deep-fried.

name: Dhokla
ingredients: 1/4 tsp Eno fruit salt or baking soda
preparation: Mix besan, yogurt, water, sugar, turmeric, oil, and salt into a smooth batter.
Just before steaming, add Eno/baking soda and mix quickly.
Pour into a greased plate/pan.
Steam for 15-18 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
For tempering: heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, chillies, water, and sugar.
Pour tempering over steamed dhokla. Cut into squares.
Garnish with coriander and serve with green chutney.
notes: Add Eno just before steaming for maximum fluffiness.

name: Khaman Dhokla
preparation: Mix besan, semolina, yogurt, water, sugar, turmeric, lemon juice, and salt.
Beat well to remove lumps. Rest for 10 minutes.
Add Eno and mix gently. Pour into greased plate.
Steam for 15-18 minutes.
For tempering: heat oil, add mustard seeds, sesame seeds, curry leaves, chillies.
Add 2 tbsp water and pour over steamed dhokla.
Cut into pieces and serve.
notes: Khaman is specifically made from besan, unlike traditional dhokla.

name: Rava Dhokla
preparation: Mix semolina, yogurt, water, sugar, and salt. Rest for 15 minutes.
Add Eno and mix gently.
Pour into greased plate and steam for 12-15 minutes.
Prepare tempering with oil, mustard seeds, curry leaves, and chillies.
Pour tempering over steamed dhokla.
Cut into squares and serve with green chutney.
notes: Rava dhokla sets faster than besan dhokla.

name: Palak Dhokla
preparation: Mix besan, spinach puree, yogurt, water, turmeric, sugar, and salt.
Beat to a smooth batter.
Add Eno and mix gently. Pour into greased plate.
Steam for 15-18 minutes.
Prepare tempering and pour over dhokla.
Cut into pieces and serve.
notes: Spinach makes these dhoklas vibrant green and nutritious.

name: Sandwich Dhokla
ingredients: For white layer: 1/2 cup besan, 1/4 cup yogurt, water, salt, Eno
For green layer: 1/2 cup besan, 1/4 cup spinach puree, yogurt, salt, Eno
preparation: Prepare white batter: mix besan, yogurt, water, salt.
Prepare green batter: mix besan, spinach puree, yogurt, salt.
Add Eno to white batter, pour in greased plate, steam 8 minutes.
Spread green chutney on top.
Add Eno to green batter, pour over white layer.
Steam another 10-12 minutes.
Prepare tempering and pour over.
Cut into squares to show layers.
notes: Two-layered dhokla with chutney in between for a festive look.

name: Khandvi
preparation: Mix besan, buttermilk, water, turmeric, ginger-chilli paste, and salt.
Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously, until thick (10-12 minutes).
Spread thin layers on the back of greased plates or trays.
Let cool slightly, then roll tightly into thin rolls.
Cut into 2-inch pieces.
Prepare tempering with oil, mustard seeds, sesame, and curry leaves.
Pour over rolls. Garnish with coconut and coriander.
notes: The key is cooking the batter to the right consistency and spreading thin.

name: Handvo
preparation: Soak rice and all dals together for 4-5 hours. Grind to a batter.
Add yogurt and ferment overnight.
Add grated bottle gourd, turmeric, sugar, green chillies, and salt.
Add Eno and mix gently.
Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds, sesame seeds, and curry leaves.
Pour batter over the tempering. Cover and cook on low for 20-25 minutes.
Flip carefully or bake at 180°C for 30 minutes.
Cut into pieces and serve with green chutney.
notes: Traditional Gujarati savory cake. Can be baked in oven too.

name: Thepla
preparation: Mix wheat flour, besan, chopped methi leaves, yogurt, turmeric, red chilli, cumin, oil, and salt.
Knead into a soft dough using water as needed. Rest 15 minutes.
Divide into balls and roll into thin circles.
Cook on hot tawa until golden on both sides.
Apply oil/ghee while cooking.
Serve with yogurt, pickle, or tea.
notes: Theplas stay fresh for days and are great for travel.

name: Methi Thepla
ingredients: 1/4 cup fresh fenugreek (methi) leaves, finely chopped
preparation: Wash and finely chop methi leaves.
Mix flour, methi, yogurt, turmeric, red chilli, cumin, sesame seeds, oil, and salt.
Knead into a soft dough. Rest 15-20 minutes.
Roll into thin circles.
Cook on tawa with oil until golden on both sides.
Serve with pickle, yogurt, or chunda.
notes: Extra methi makes this more flavorful. Classic Gujarati travel food.

name: Bajra Thepla
preparation: Mix bajra flour, wheat flour, methi leaves, yogurt, spices, oil, and salt.
Knead into a dough. Rest 15 minutes.
Divide into balls and pat/roll into circles (bajra dough is less elastic).
Cook on tawa with ghee until golden on both sides.
Serve hot with white butter and jaggery.
notes: Bajra thepla is warming and traditional in winter.

name: Appam
ingredients: 1/12 tsp dry yeast or 1/4 cup toddy
preparation: Grind soaked rice with coconut, cooked rice, and water to a smooth batter.
Add sugar, salt, and yeast. Mix well.
Ferment for 6-8 hours until bubbly.
Add a little water if batter is too thick - it should be pouring consistency.
Heat an appam pan (appachatti). Pour a ladleful of batter.
Swirl the pan to spread batter thin on the sides while center remains thick.
Cover and cook for 2-3 minutes. Edges should be lacy and crisp.
Serve with vegetable stew or coconut milk.
notes: Swirl the pan quickly for thin, lacy edges with a soft center.

name: Egg Appam
preparation: Heat appam pan and grease with coconut oil.
Pour batter and swirl to coat the sides.
Crack an egg into the thick center of the appam.
Sprinkle salt and pepper on the egg.
Cover and cook for 3-4 minutes until egg white is set.
Serve hot.
notes: The egg cooks in the center of the appam. Great protein boost.

name: Stew Appam
ingredients: For appam: use standard appam batter
preparation: Make appams as per standard recipe.
For stew: heat coconut oil, add whole spices and curry leaves.
Add onion and cook until soft. Add green chillies.
Add vegetables and thin coconut milk. Cook until vegetables are tender.
Add thick coconut milk and salt. Simmer for 5 minutes (do not boil).
Serve appams with warm stew.
notes: Don't boil after adding thick coconut milk or it may curdle.

name: Coconut Appam
preparation: Grind soaked rice with extra coconut, cooked rice, and water to a smooth batter.
Add sugar, salt, and yeast. Mix well. Ferment 6-8 hours.
Heat appam pan, grease with coconut oil.
Pour batter and swirl the pan.
Cover and cook 2-3 minutes until edges are crisp.
Serve with coconut chutney or stew.
notes: Extra coconut makes the appam richer and more fragrant.

name: Paal Appam
preparation: Make or use pre-made appams.
Heat coconut milk in a pan. Add sugar/jaggery and cardamom.
Stir until sugar dissolves. Do not boil vigorously.
Tear appams into pieces and soak in the sweet coconut milk.
Drizzle ghee on top.
Serve warm as a sweet breakfast.
notes: Paal means milk in Tamil. A sweet, comforting breakfast.

name: Puttu
preparation: Mix rice flour with salt.
Sprinkle water little by little and mix until the flour is moist and crumbly (not doughy).
It should hold shape when pressed but crumble easily.
Layer grated coconut and moistened flour alternately in a puttu maker.
Steam for 5-7 minutes.
Push out the puttu and serve with banana, kadala curry, or sugar.
notes: The flour must be crumbly, not wet. This is key for soft puttu.

name: Wheat Puttu
preparation: Mix wheat flour with salt.
Sprinkle water gradually and mix until crumbly and moist.
Layer coconut and wheat flour alternately in puttu maker.
Steam for 7-8 minutes.
Push out and serve with banana and sugar or kadala curry.
notes: Wheat puttu is healthier with more fiber than rice puttu.

name: Ragi Puttu
preparation: Mix ragi flour with salt.
Sprinkle water and mix until crumbly.
Layer coconut and ragi flour in puttu maker.
Steam for 7-8 minutes.
Push out and serve with banana and jaggery.
notes: Ragi puttu is rich in calcium and gives a dark, earthy color.

name: Rice Puttu
preparation: Take fine rice flour and add salt.
Sprinkle water and mix to a crumbly texture.
Layer grated coconut and rice flour in puttu maker.
Steam for 5-7 minutes.
Serve hot with kadala curry, egg curry, or with banana and sugar.
notes: Classic Kerala breakfast. Use fine rice flour for smooth texture.

name: Chiratta Puttu
preparation: Prepare rice flour with water and salt to a crumbly texture.
Use a coconut shell half as the mold.
Layer coconut and rice flour in the coconut shell.
Steam in a steamer for 5-7 minutes.
Unmold the dome-shaped puttu.
Serve with banana and kadala curry.
notes: Chiratta means coconut shell - gives a traditional dome shape.

name: Aloo Tikki
preparation: Mash boiled potatoes smoothly. Add cornflour, all spices, chillies, coriander, and salt.
Mix well and shape into round flat tikkis.
Heat oil in a pan for shallow frying.
Place tikkis and cook on medium heat until golden and crispy on both sides.
Drain on paper towels.
Serve with mint chutney and tamarind chutney.
notes: Adding cornflour helps the tikkis hold shape and get crispy.

name: Corn Tikki
preparation: Coarsely mash or pulse boiled corn kernels (keep some texture).
Mix with mashed potato, cornflour, chilli, cumin, coriander, and salt.
Shape into flat round tikkis.
Shallow fry on medium heat until golden on both sides.
Serve with green chutney.
notes: Keep corn slightly chunky for a nice texture contrast.

name: Mixed Veg Tikki
ingredients: 1/16 cup French beans, finely chopped and boiled
preparation: Mix mashed potato with all boiled vegetables.
Add cornflour, spices, chillies, coriander, and salt.
Shape into flat tikkis.
Shallow fry until golden and crispy on both sides.
Serve with mint-coriander chutney.
notes: Make sure vegetables are well-drained to prevent tikkis from breaking.

name: Beetroot Tikki
preparation: Squeeze excess water from grated beetroot.
Mix beetroot with mashed potato, cornflour, spices, chillies, coriander, and salt.
Shape into flat round tikkis.
Shallow fry until crispy on both sides.
Serve with yogurt dip or green chutney.
notes: Beetroot gives a beautiful pink color. Squeeze out water well.

name: Sweet Potato Tikki
ingredients: 1/2 large sweet potatoes, boiled and mashed
preparation: Mash boiled sweet potatoes. Add cornflour, all spices, coriander, and salt.
Mix well and shape into flat tikkis.
Shallow fry on medium heat until golden on both sides.
Serve with tamarind chutney or yogurt.
notes: Sweet potato adds natural sweetness and more nutrients.

name: Masala Oats
preparation: Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and chillies, saute until soft.
Add tomato and vegetables. Cook 2-3 minutes.
Add turmeric, water, and salt. Bring to a boil.
Add oats and mix well.
Cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Quick and healthy breakfast ready in 10 minutes.

name: Vegetable Oats Porridge
preparation: Heat oil, add cumin seeds and curry leaves.
Add all vegetables and saute 2-3 minutes.
Add water, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil.
Add oats and stir well.
Cook on low heat for 4-5 minutes until thick and creamy.
Serve hot.
notes: A warm, comforting porridge-style breakfast.

name: Banana Oats
preparation: Cook oats with milk on medium heat for 3-4 minutes.
Add mashed banana and honey. Mix well.
Add cinnamon and cook for another minute.
Top with sliced almonds and raisins.
Serve warm.
notes: Use a ripe banana for natural sweetness.

name: Apple Cinnamon Oats
preparation: Heat ghee and saute diced apple for 2 minutes.
Add milk and bring to a simmer.
Add oats and cinnamon. Cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add honey and mix well.
Top with chopped walnuts and serve warm.
notes: Cinnamon and apple is a classic warming combination.

name: Poori Bhaji
ingredients: For pooris: 2 cups whole wheat flour, 1 tbsp oil, salt, water
For bhaji: 4 potatoes boiled and mashed, 1 onion chopped, 1 tsp mustard seeds, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 2 green chillies, 8 curry leaves, 1 tbsp oil, salt
preparation: Knead flour with oil, salt, and water into a stiff dough. Rest 15 minutes.
For bhaji: heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and chillies, cook until soft.
Add turmeric and mashed potato. Mix well, add salt.
Roll dough into small circles.
Deep fry in hot oil until pooris puff up and turn golden.
Serve hot pooris with potato bhaji.
notes: Oil should be hot enough for pooris to puff immediately.

name: Bedmi Poori
ingredients: For pooris: 1.5 cups whole wheat flour, 1/2 cup urad dal soaked and ground, 1/2 tsp cumin seeds, 1/4 tsp asafoetida, 1/2 tsp red chilli powder, salt, oil for frying
For aloo sabzi: 3 potatoes, 1 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tomato, salt
preparation: Grind soaked urad dal to a coarse paste.
Mix flour, dal paste, cumin, asafoetida, red chilli, and salt. Knead into dough.
For sabzi: boil and mash potatoes. Cook with cumin, turmeric, coriander, tomato, salt.
Roll pooris slightly thick.
Deep fry in hot oil until puffed and golden.
Serve with aloo sabzi.
notes: Bedmi pooris are stuffed with dal and are a Delhi specialty.

name: Chole Bhature Light
ingredients: For bhature: 1 cup maida, 1/4 cup yogurt, 1/4 tsp baking soda, 1 tbsp oil, salt, water
For chole: 1 cup chickpeas soaked overnight, 1 onion, 2 tomatoes, 1 tbsp chole masala, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp red chilli, 1 inch ginger, 2 green chillies, 1 tbsp oil, salt
preparation: For chole: pressure cook chickpeas until soft.
Heat oil, add ginger, chillies, onion. Cook until golden.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Add all spices.
Add chickpeas with some cooking water. Simmer 15 minutes.
For bhature: mix flour, yogurt, baking soda, oil, salt. Knead soft dough. Rest 2 hours.
Roll into ovals and deep fry until puffed and golden.
Serve bhature with chole, onion rings, and pickle.
notes: Rest the bhatura dough well for soft, fluffy results.

name: Pav Bhaji Light
preparation: Boil and mash cauliflower, peas, carrot, and potatoes.
Heat butter, add onion and cook until golden.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add pav bhaji masala, turmeric, and salt.
Add mashed vegetables. Mix well and mash further.
Add water as needed. Cook for 10 minutes.
Toast pav buns with butter on a tawa.
Serve bhaji with toasted pav, lemon, and onion.
notes: Mash the vegetables well for a smooth bhaji.

name: Medu Vada
preparation: Drain urad dal and grind to a thick, fluffy batter with minimal water.
Add ginger, chillies, curry leaves, pepper, asafoetida, and salt.
Wet your hands, take a portion of batter, and shape into a donut.
Use your thumb to make a hole in the center.
Slide into hot oil and fry on medium heat.
Fry until golden brown on both sides.
Drain and serve with coconut chutney and sambar.
notes: Batter must be thick and fluffy - don't add too much water while grinding.

name: Dal Vada
preparation: Drain both dals and grind coarsely - do not make a smooth paste.
Add onion, chillies, ginger, curry leaves, coriander, asafoetida, and salt.
Shape into flat round patties.
Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy.
Drain on paper towels.
Serve with coconut chutney.
notes: Coarse grinding gives the vada its characteristic crunchy texture.

name: Masala Vada
preparation: Drain chana dal and grind coarsely with chillies and ginger.
Add onion, curry leaves, coriander, red chilli powder, asafoetida, and salt.
Mix well. Shape into flat patties.
Deep fry in hot oil on medium heat until golden brown.
Drain and serve with chutney or as a snack.
notes: Also called paruppu vadai in Tamil Nadu.

name: Sabudana Vada Plain
preparation: Drain soaked sabudana well.
Mix with mashed potato, ground peanuts, chillies, and salt.
Shape into round flat patties.
Shallow fry in oil until golden on both sides.
Serve hot with curd or green chutney.
notes: Popular during fasting (vrat). Use sendha namak for fasting.

name: Misal Pav
ingredients: For usal: 1 onion, 1 tomato, 1 tsp misal masala, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp oil, salt
For topping: farsan/sev, chopped onion, coriander, lemon wedges
preparation: Pressure cook sprouts until soft.
Heat oil, add onion and cook until golden.
Add tomato and cook. Add misal masala, turmeric, salt.
Add sprouts with water. Simmer 10 minutes.
Serve in a bowl, top with farsan, onion, coriander.
Toast pav with butter and serve alongside with lemon.
notes: Spicy Maharashtrian breakfast. Adjust chilli to taste.

name: Zunka Bhakri
ingredients: For zunka: 1 cup besan, 1 onion chopped, 2 green chillies, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp mustard seeds, 8 curry leaves, 1 tbsp oil, salt
For bhakri: 2 cups jowar flour, water, salt
preparation: For bhakri: mix jowar flour with water and salt. Pat into flat rounds. Cook on tawa.
For zunka: heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves.
Add onion and chillies, saute.
Add turmeric and besan. Stir continuously on low heat.
Cook for 8-10 minutes until besan is cooked and crumbly.
Serve zunka with hot bhakri and green chilli.
notes: Traditional Maharashtrian rural breakfast. Simple and nutritious.

name: Thalipeeth
preparation: Mix all flours together.
Add onion, chillies, coriander, cumin, turmeric, and salt.
Add water gradually and make a soft dough.
Take a portion and pat directly on a greased tawa into a flat round.
Make a small hole in the center. Pour oil in the hole and around edges.
Cook on medium heat until golden. Flip and cook other side.
Serve with butter, yogurt, or pickle.
notes: Multi-grain Maharashtrian flatbread. Pat directly on tawa, don't roll.

name: Jowar Bhakri
preparation: Add salt to jowar flour.
Add hot water gradually and knead into a dough (jowar dough needs hot water).
Divide into balls.
Pat each ball flat on a surface dusted with jowar flour (or pat between palms).
Cook on hot tawa until brown spots appear on both sides.
Apply ghee and serve hot with pitla, zunka, or any sabzi.
notes: Must use hot water for jowar dough. Pat gently as it cracks easily.

name: Uttapam
preparation: Heat a tawa on medium heat, grease with oil.
Pour a ladleful of thick batter and spread into a thick circle (not thin like dosa).
Sprinkle onion, tomato, and coriander on top.
Press toppings gently into the batter.
Drizzle oil around edges. Cook on medium heat.
Flip carefully and cook the other side until golden.
Serve with coconut chutney and sambar.
notes: Use thick batter for soft, spongy uttapam.

name: Onion Uttapam
preparation: Heat tawa, grease with oil.
Pour thick batter and spread into a circle.
Top generously with chopped onions, chillies, and coriander.
Press toppings into batter. Drizzle oil.
Cook until bottom is golden. Flip and cook other side.
Serve with chutney and sambar.
notes: The most popular uttapam variant.

name: Tomato Uttapam
preparation: Heat tawa, grease with oil.
Pour batter and spread thick.
Top with chopped tomatoes, onion, chillies, and coriander.
Press gently, drizzle oil.
Cook until golden on bottom, flip and cook other side.
Serve with coconut chutney.
notes: Juicy tomatoes add a tangy flavor.

name: Mixed Veg Uttapam
preparation: Mix all chopped vegetables together.
Heat tawa, grease with oil.
Pour thick batter and spread.
Sprinkle mixed vegetables generously on top.
Press gently, drizzle oil.
Cook until golden, flip and cook other side.
Serve with chutney and sambar.
notes: Colorful and nutritious with all the vegetables.

name: Egg Bhurji
preparation: Heat oil, add cumin seeds.
Add onion and saute until golden.
Add green chillies and tomato. Cook until tomato is soft.
Add turmeric, red chilli powder, and salt.
Crack eggs directly into the pan.
Scramble continuously until eggs are cooked but still moist.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with pav or roti.
notes: Indian-style scrambled eggs. Don't overcook for best texture.

name: Masala Egg Toast
preparation: Beat eggs with onion, chilli, coriander, turmeric, and salt.
Dip bread slices in egg mixture, coating both sides.
Heat butter on a tawa.
Place coated bread and cook until golden on both sides.
Serve hot with ketchup or green chutney.
notes: Indian-style French toast. Great for a quick breakfast.

name: Egg Paratha
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt into soft dough. Rest 15 minutes.
Roll into thin parathas.
Cook paratha on tawa until half done.
Beat eggs with onion, chillies, coriander, turmeric, and salt.
Pour egg mixture on the paratha, flip immediately.
Cook both sides with ghee until golden and egg is set.
Serve hot with ketchup or chutney.
notes: Egg is spread on the paratha while cooking on the tawa.

name: Egg Dosa
preparation: Heat tawa, pour batter and spread into a thin dosa.
Crack an egg on top of the dosa.
Spread the egg over the dosa surface with the back of a spoon.
Sprinkle onion, chillies, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle oil. Cook until dosa is crisp and egg is set.
Fold and serve with chutney.
notes: Break the egg directly on the dosa for even distribution.

name: Paneer Bhurji
preparation: Heat butter, add cumin seeds.
Add onion and saute until golden.
Add capsicum, green chillies, and tomato. Cook 2-3 minutes.
Add turmeric, red chilli powder, and salt.
Add crumbled paneer and mix well.
Cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with roti or pav.
notes: Vegetarian alternative to egg bhurji. Quick and delicious.

name: Paneer Tikka Toast
preparation: Marinate paneer slices in yogurt, red chilli, turmeric, cumin, chaat masala, and salt for 15 minutes.
Grill marinated paneer on a tawa until golden on both sides.
Butter bread slices and toast.
Place grilled paneer on toast.
Sprinkle chaat masala and coriander.
Serve hot.
notes: Marinate paneer for at least 15 minutes for best flavor.

name: Paneer Roll
preparation: Heat oil, saute paneer strips until lightly golden. Set aside.
Saute onion and capsicum briefly.
Warm rotis on tawa.
Spread mint chutney on each roti.
Place paneer strips, onion, and capsicum.
Sprinkle chaat masala and lemon juice.
Roll tightly and serve.
notes: Use warm rotis for easier rolling.

name: Mung Bean Sprout Chaat
preparation: Steam moong sprouts for 8-10 minutes until tender.
Let cool slightly.
Add onion, tomato, cucumber, chillies, and coriander.
Add chaat masala, cumin powder, lemon juice, and salt.
Toss well.
Top with sev and serve immediately.
notes: High-protein breakfast. Add pomegranate for extra taste.

name: Sprouted Moong Salad
preparation: Lightly steam or blanch sprouts (or use raw if preferred).
Mix with cucumber, carrot, tomato, and pomegranate.
Add coriander, chaat masala, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Toss well and serve fresh.
notes: Light, refreshing, and protein-rich breakfast option.

name: Ragi Porridge
preparation: Mix ragi flour with water to make a lump-free slurry.
Bring milk to a boil.
Slowly add the ragi slurry to boiling milk, stirring continuously.
Cook on low heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add jaggery and cardamom. Mix well.
Serve warm.
notes: Stir constantly to prevent lumps. Rich in calcium and iron.

name: Ragi Dosa Plain
preparation: Mix ragi flour and rice flour.
Add water to make a thin batter.
Add onion, cumin, curry leaves, chillies, and salt.
Heat tawa, pour batter and spread thin.
Drizzle oil and cook until crisp.
Serve with coconut chutney.
notes: No fermentation needed. Quick and nutritious.

name: Ragi Mudde Light
preparation: Boil 1.5 cups water with salt.
Mix ragi flour with remaining 1/2 cup cold water to make a slurry.
Pour slurry into boiling water, stirring continuously.
Cook on low heat, stirring, until it forms a thick mass.
Wet your hands and shape into smooth round balls.
Serve with sambar, rasam, or any dal.
notes: Traditional Karnataka staple. Must be eaten fresh and warm.

name: Quinoa Upma
preparation: Rinse quinoa well to remove bitterness.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and chillies, saute until soft.
Add vegetables and turmeric, cook 2 minutes.
Add water, salt, and quinoa. Bring to a boil.
Cover and cook on low for 15 minutes until quinoa is fluffy.
Fluff with a fork, add lemon juice, and serve.
notes: Quinoa is a complete protein. Rinse well to remove saponins.

name: Quinoa Poha
preparation: Cook quinoa and let it cool.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add peanuts, onion, and chillies. Saute until onion is soft.
Add turmeric and cooked quinoa.
Mix gently and cook 2-3 minutes.
Add lemon juice, garnish with coriander.
notes: Healthy substitute for regular poha with more protein.

name: Quinoa Idli
preparation: Soak quinoa, rice, and urad dal separately for 6 hours.
Grind urad dal to a fluffy batter. Grind quinoa and rice together.
Mix both batters, add salt, and ferment overnight.
Pour into greased idli molds.
Steam for 12-15 minutes.
Serve with chutney and sambar.
notes: Quinoa adds extra protein and nutrients to regular idli.

name: Bajra Roti
preparation: Add salt to bajra flour.
Add hot water gradually and knead into a soft dough.
Divide into balls. Pat each into a flat round (bajra doesn't roll well).
Cook on hot tawa until brown spots appear. Flip and cook other side.
Puff over direct flame if desired.
Apply ghee and serve hot.
notes: Use hot water for pliable dough. Best served fresh with ghee.

name: Nachni Dosa
preparation: Mix nachni flour and rice flour.
Add water to make thin batter. Add onion, chillies, cumin, curry leaves, salt.
Rest 10 minutes.
Heat tawa, pour batter and spread.
Drizzle oil and cook until crisp.
Serve with chutney.
notes: Nachni is another name for ragi in Maharashtra.

name: Jowar Dosa
preparation: Mix jowar and rice flour.
Add water to make thin batter.
Add onion, chillies, curry leaves, cumin, and salt.
Heat tawa, pour batter and spread.
Cook until crisp on bottom. No need to flip.
Serve with green chutney.
notes: No fermentation needed. Gluten-free option.

name: Sattu Drink
preparation: Add sattu to cold water and mix well.
Add lemon juice, cumin powder, black salt, and salt.
Stir vigorously until well combined.
Add ice if desired.
Garnish with mint leaves.
Drink immediately as sattu settles.
notes: Bihari superfood drink. Very cooling in summer. Stir before drinking.

name: Sattu Paratha
ingredients: For stuffing: 1 cup sattu, 1 small onion finely chopped, 2 green chillies chopped, 2 tbsp fresh coriander, 1 tsp ajwain, 1 tbsp mustard oil, juice of half lemon, salt
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt into soft dough. Rest 20 minutes.
Mix sattu with onion, chillies, coriander, ajwain, mustard oil, lemon juice, and salt.
Roll dough, stuff with sattu filling, seal, and roll out.
Cook on tawa with ghee until golden on both sides.
Serve with pickle and yogurt.
notes: Sattu paratha is a protein-rich Bihari specialty.

name: Sattu Chilla
preparation: Mix sattu with water to make a thin batter.
Add onion, chillies, coriander, ajwain, and salt.
Heat non-stick pan, grease with oil.
Pour batter and spread into a thin circle.
Cook until golden on both sides.
Serve with mint chutney.
notes: High-protein chilla from Bihar. No fermentation needed.

name: Pongal
preparation: Dry roast moong dal until aromatic. Wash rice and dal.
Pressure cook rice and dal with water for 3-4 whistles until mushy.
Heat ghee, add cumin, pepper, curry leaves, and cashews.
Add ginger and asafoetida. Saute briefly.
Pour tempering into the cooked rice-dal mixture.
Mix well, add salt. Consistency should be like porridge.
Serve hot with sambar and coconut chutney.
notes: Classic Tamil Nadu comfort food. Must be creamy and peppery.

name: Ven Pongal
preparation: Dry roast moong dal. Wash and cook rice and dal together until very soft.
Heat generous ghee, add cumin, pepper, curry leaves, and cashews.
Add ginger and asafoetida.
Pour into the rice-dal mixture. Mix thoroughly.
Add salt. The pongal should be creamy.
Serve hot with sambar and coconut chutney.
notes: Ven means white. Extra ghee and pepper are the hallmarks.

name: Akki Roti
preparation: Mix rice flour with all vegetables, coconut, spices, and salt.
Add water gradually and make a soft dough.
Take a portion and pat directly on a greased banana leaf or greased tawa.
Make a thin round circle. Poke a small hole in center.
Pour oil in hole and around edges.
Cook on medium heat until golden. Flip and cook other side.
Serve with coconut chutney and pickle.
notes: Karnataka special. Pat on banana leaf for authentic preparation.

name: Rice Roti
preparation: Mix rice flour with salt.
Add hot water and knead into a soft dough.
Add onion, chillies, coriander, and cumin.
Pat into thin round rotis (rice flour doesn't roll easily).
Cook on tawa with oil until golden on both sides.
Serve with chutney or pickle.
notes: Use hot water for pliable rice dough.

name: Neer Dosa Plain
preparation: Grind soaked rice with coconut and water to a very thin batter.
Add salt. Batter should be like thin buttermilk.
Heat a non-stick pan.
Pour batter and swirl to coat.
Cook 1-2 minutes. Dosa will be thin and lacy.
Fold and serve with chicken curry or coconut chutney.
notes: Neer means water. Batter must be very thin.

name: Curd Rice Bowl
preparation: Mash cooked rice slightly. Add yogurt and milk. Mix well.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, and curry leaves.
Add chillies and ginger. Pour tempering over rice.
Add grated carrot, cucumber, and salt. Mix well.
Garnish with pomegranate seeds.
Serve at room temperature or slightly chilled.
notes: Cooling and probiotic-rich. Great for summer mornings.

name: Dahi Chivda
preparation: Rinse thin poha briefly and drain.
Mix with yogurt, sugar, and salt.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, and chillies.
Pour tempering over the poha-yogurt mixture.
Add peanuts and coriander. Mix well.
Top with sev and serve.
notes: Quick Maharashtra-style breakfast. Serve immediately before sev gets soggy.

name: Poha Jalebi Light
ingredients: For poha: 1 cup poha, 1 onion, peanuts, mustard seeds, curry leaves, turmeric, salt, oil
For light jalebi: 1/2 cup maida, 1/4 cup yogurt, sugar syrup, saffron, oil for frying
preparation: Make standard masala poha with mustard seeds, curry leaves, peanuts, onion, turmeric.
For jalebi: mix maida and yogurt. Ferment 4 hours.
Make sugar syrup with saffron.
Pipe batter into hot oil in spiral shapes. Fry until crisp.
Dip in sugar syrup briefly.
Serve poha with 2-3 jalebis on the side.
notes: Classic Indore combo. Light version uses less sugar syrup.

name: Sevai Upma
preparation: Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and chillies, saute until soft.
Add vegetables and turmeric. Cook 2-3 minutes.
Add water and salt, bring to a boil.
Add rice sevai. Mix gently.
Cook covered on low for 3-4 minutes.
Add lemon juice and serve.
notes: Rice sevai cooks very quickly. Don't overcook.

name: Lemon Sevai
preparation: Steam or boil sevai until soft. Drain and set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, chana dal, and peanuts.
Add curry leaves and green chillies.
Add turmeric and salt.
Add cooked sevai and mix gently.
Add lemon juice and toss well.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: Tangy and light. Add lemon juice after turning off heat.

name: Coconut Sevai
preparation: Steam or boil sevai until soft. Drain.
Heat coconut oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, and curry leaves.
Add green chillies and saute.
Add grated coconut and mix.
Add cooked sevai and salt. Toss gently.
Serve warm.
notes: Use fresh coconut for the best flavor.

name: Masala Toast
preparation: Mix mashed potato with onion, chillies, coriander, chaat masala, and salt.
Spread potato mixture on bread slices.
Cover with another bread slice.
Butter both sides.
Toast on a tawa or sandwich maker until golden and crispy.
Cut diagonally and serve with ketchup or green chutney.
notes: Indian-style grilled sandwich. Press while toasting for crispiness.

name: Bombay Sandwich Light
preparation: Spread green chutney on bread slices.
Layer potato, cucumber, tomato, onion, and beetroot.
Sprinkle chaat masala and salt.
Close with another bread slice.
Butter outside and toast on tawa until golden.
Cut into triangles and serve with ketchup.
notes: Iconic Mumbai street food. Green chutney is essential.

name: Dabeli Light
preparation: Heat oil, add mashed potato and dabeli masala. Mix well. Cook 2-3 minutes.
Slit pav buns horizontally.
Spread tamarind chutney and garlic chutney inside.
Stuff with spiced potato mixture.
Top with pomegranate, peanuts, and sev.
Toast on tawa with a little butter.
Serve hot.
notes: Kutchi Gujarati street food. The chutneys make it special.

name: Anda Ghotala
preparation: Heat butter on a tawa or flat pan.
Add onion, cook until golden. Add chillies.
Add tomato, pav bhaji masala, turmeric, and red chilli.
Cook until tomato is mashed.
Crack eggs directly on the tawa.
Scramble and mix with the masala continuously.
Cook until eggs are done but still moist.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with buttered pav.
notes: Mumbai street-style spicy scrambled eggs. Cooked on a flat tawa.

name: Egg Fried Rice
preparation: Heat oil in a wok/pan on high heat.
Scramble eggs and set aside.
Add onion, carrot, and peas. Stir-fry 2-3 minutes.
Add cold rice and stir-fry on high heat.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, and salt.
Add scrambled eggs back. Toss well.
Garnish with green onions and serve.
notes: Use day-old rice for best texture. High heat is key.

name: Egg Maggi Healthy
preparation: Heat oil, add onion and chilli. Saute briefly.
Add vegetables and cook 2 minutes.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Add noodles and tastemaker. Cook 3-4 minutes.
Crack an egg on top. Stir gently to mix.
Cook until egg is set and noodles are done.
Serve hot.
notes: Added vegetables and egg make instant noodles more nutritious.

name: Pakhala
preparation: Soak cooked rice in water overnight or for a few hours.
Add yogurt and salt. Mix well.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, dried chillies, asafoetida.
Pour tempering over the rice.
Mix well and serve at room temperature.
Serve with fried vegetables or pickle.
notes: Odia fermented rice. Very cooling. Traditional summer breakfast.

name: Pesarattu Plain
ingredients: 1/4 cup green moong dal, soaked 4-5 hours
preparation: Grind soaked moong dal with ginger and chillies to a smooth batter.
Add salt. No fermentation needed.
Heat tawa, pour batter and spread thin.
Drizzle oil and cook until crisp.
Serve with ginger chutney.
notes: Andhra specialty. High protein and no fermentation needed.

name: Pesarattu Upma
ingredients: For pesarattu: 1 cup green moong dal, ginger, chillies, salt
For upma filling: 1/2 cup semolina, 1 onion, curry leaves, mustard seeds, chillies, salt, oil
preparation: Make pesarattu (green moong dosa) as per standard recipe.
Prepare semolina upma separately.
Place a portion of upma on the pesarattu.
Fold the pesarattu over the upma.
Serve with ginger chutney.
notes: Pesarattu with upma inside is an iconic Andhra combo.

name: Kozhukattai Savory
ingredients: For filling: 1/2 cup grated coconut, 2 tbsp chana dal roasted, 2 green chillies, 1 tsp mustard seeds, curry leaves
preparation: Boil water with salt and oil. Add rice flour and stir quickly.
Knead into smooth dough. Keep covered.
For filling: temper mustard seeds, curry leaves. Add coconut, chana dal, chillies, salt.
Take a small dough ball, flatten, place filling, seal into a dumpling.
Steam for 10-12 minutes.
Serve with coconut chutney.
notes: Tamil Nadu steamed dumplings. Shape by cupping in palm.

name: Ellu Seedai Light
preparation: Mix rice flour, urad dal flour, sesame seeds, butter, and salt.
Add water gradually and knead into a firm dough.
Shape into small round balls.
Deep fry on low-medium heat until golden and crispy.
Drain and cool.
Serve as a crunchy breakfast snack.
notes: Traditional Tamil Nadu snack. Fry on low heat for even cooking.

name: Murukku Light
preparation: Mix rice flour, urad dal flour, butter, cumin, asafoetida, and salt.
Add water and knead into a smooth dough.
Use a murukku press to pipe spirals directly into hot oil.
Fry on medium heat until golden and crisp.
Drain and cool completely.
Store in airtight container.
notes: Crispy South Indian snack. Press directly into oil for best shape.

name: Chole Kulche Light
ingredients: For chole: 1 cup chickpeas soaked overnight, 1 onion, 2 tomatoes, 1 tbsp chole masala, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 inch ginger, salt, oil
For kulche: 2 cups maida, 1/4 cup yogurt, 1/4 tsp baking soda, salt, oil
preparation: Pressure cook chickpeas. Make chole gravy with onion, tomato, and spices.
Knead kulcha dough with maida, yogurt, baking soda, salt. Rest 2 hours.
Roll into small ovals.
Cook kulchas on tawa until puffy and golden, or bake in oven.
Serve hot kulchas with chole, onion slices, and lemon.
notes: Delhi street food classic. Rest the dough well for soft kulchas.

name: Rajma Chawal Mini
preparation: Pressure cook soaked rajma until soft (5-6 whistles).
Heat oil, add ginger, onion. Cook until golden.
Add tomato and cook until soft.
Add spices and salt. Cook 2 minutes.
Add cooked rajma with some cooking water.
Simmer for 10-15 minutes until thick.
Serve over rice with coriander.
notes: Smaller portion as breakfast. Classic North Indian comfort food.

name: Aloo Bonda Light
ingredients: For filling: 3 potatoes boiled and mashed, 1 tsp mustard seeds, 8 curry leaves, 2 green chillies, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 inch ginger, salt, 1 tbsp oil
For batter: 1 cup besan, 1/4 tsp turmeric, pinch of baking soda, salt, water
preparation: Heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, chillies, ginger.
Add turmeric and mashed potato. Mix well with salt. Cool.
Make besan batter with turmeric, soda, salt, and water.
Shape potato into round balls.
Dip in besan batter and deep fry until golden.
Drain and serve with coconut chutney.
notes: South Indian style potato fritter. Light version uses less oil.

name: Corn Poha
preparation: Rinse poha and drain. Add turmeric and salt.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add corn kernels and cook 3-4 minutes.
Add peanuts, onion, and chillies. Saute until onion is soft.
Add poha and mix gently. Cook 3-4 minutes.
Add lemon juice, garnish with coriander.
notes: Sweet corn adds a lovely crunch and sweetness.

name: Mushroom Paratha
ingredients: For filling: 200g mushrooms finely chopped, 1 onion finely chopped, 2 green chillies, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp garam masala, coriander, salt, 1 tbsp oil
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt. Rest 20 minutes.
Heat oil, saute onion until golden. Add mushrooms and cook until dry.
Add spices, chillies, coriander, salt. Cool the filling.
Roll dough, stuff with mushroom filling, seal, roll out.
Cook on tawa with ghee until golden on both sides.
Serve with yogurt.
notes: Cook mushrooms until all moisture evaporates.

name: Palak Poori
preparation: Blanch spinach, cool, and puree.
Mix flour with spinach puree, cumin, ajwain, and salt.
Knead into a firm dough. Rest 15 minutes.
Roll into small circles.
Deep fry in hot oil until puffed and golden.
Serve with aloo sabzi or pickle.
notes: Spinach gives beautiful green color and extra nutrition.

name: Beetroot Paratha
preparation: Mix flour with grated beetroot, chilli, cumin, garam masala, coriander, and salt.
Knead into a pink dough using minimal water. Rest 15 minutes.
Roll into circles.
Cook on tawa with ghee until golden on both sides.
Serve with yogurt and pickle.
notes: Beetroot gives a vibrant pink color. Kids love it.

name: Carrot Paratha
preparation: Mix flour with grated carrot, chilli, cumin, garam masala, coriander, and salt.
Knead into dough using minimal water (carrot releases moisture). Rest 15 minutes.
Roll into circles.
Cook on tawa with ghee until golden on both sides.
Serve with yogurt and pickle.
notes: Naturally sweet from carrots. Great for kids.

name: Curd Dosa
preparation: Mix leftover dosa batter with yogurt. Adjust salt.
Add onion, chillies, and coriander.
Heat tawa, grease with oil.
Pour batter and spread. Cook until golden on both sides.
Serve with coconut chutney.
notes: Great way to use over-fermented sour dosa batter.

name: Rice Flour Dosa
preparation: Mix rice flour with yogurt and water to make thin batter.
Add onion, chillies, cumin, and salt. Rest 10 minutes.
Heat tawa, pour batter and spread.
Cook until crisp. Flip if desired.
Serve with chutney.
notes: No fermentation needed. Instant dosa recipe.

name: Classic Oatmeal
preparation: Bring milk/water to a boil.
Add oats and reduce heat.
Cook for 3-5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add honey and cinnamon.
Top with berries and nuts. Serve warm.
notes: Classic comfort breakfast. Adjust milk for desired consistency.

name: Steel Cut Oats
preparation: Bring water to a boil. Add steel cut oats.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add milk in the last 5 minutes.
Add honey and cinnamon.
Top with fresh fruit and nuts.
notes: Steel cut oats take longer but have a chewier, nuttier texture.

name: Overnight Oats
preparation: Mix oats, yogurt, milk, chia seeds, and honey in a jar.
Stir well, cover, and refrigerate overnight.
In the morning, top with berries and almond butter.
Eat cold or warm slightly if preferred.
notes: Prepare the night before for a grab-and-go breakfast.

name: Berry Oatmeal Bowl
preparation: Cook oats with milk for 3-5 minutes.
Add honey and stir.
Top with mixed berries, chia seeds, and almonds.
Serve warm.
notes: Berries add antioxidants and natural sweetness.

name: Avocado Toast
preparation: Toast bread slices until golden.
Mash avocado with lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Spread mashed avocado on toast.
Drizzle olive oil and sprinkle chilli flakes.
Serve immediately.
notes: Use ripe avocado for easy mashing. Add toppings as desired.

name: Smashed Avocado Rye Toast
preparation: Toast rye bread.
Mash avocado roughly (keep some texture).
Mix with lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Spread on rye toast.
Top with diced tomato, onion, and microgreens.
Sprinkle chilli flakes and serve.
notes: Rye bread adds extra fiber and a deeper flavor.

name: Avocado Egg Toast
preparation: Toast bread. Mash avocado with salt and pepper.
Poach eggs in simmering water with vinegar for 3-4 minutes.
Spread avocado on toast.
Place poached egg on top.
Sprinkle salt, pepper, and chilli flakes. Drizzle olive oil.
notes: Poached egg should have runny yolk for best experience.

name: Greek Yogurt Parfait
preparation: Layer yogurt in a glass or bowl.
Add a layer of granola.
Add berries.
Repeat layers.
Drizzle honey on top.
Sprinkle chia seeds and almonds. Serve immediately.
notes: Layer just before eating so granola stays crunchy.

name: Honey Granola Bowl
preparation: Pour granola into a bowl.
Add milk or yogurt.
Top with banana slices, pumpkin seeds, and cranberries.
Drizzle honey. Serve immediately.
notes: Choose low-sugar granola for a healthier option.

name: Mixed Berry Granola
preparation: Place yogurt in a bowl.
Top with granola and mixed berries.
Sprinkle flax seeds.
Drizzle honey and serve.
notes: Rich in antioxidants and probiotics.

name: Banana Pancakes
preparation: Mash bananas. Mix with eggs, oat flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and honey.
Heat butter in a non-stick pan on medium-low.
Pour small rounds of batter.
Cook 2-3 minutes per side until golden.
Serve with maple syrup and fresh fruit.
notes: Use very ripe bananas for natural sweetness.

name: Oat Flour Pancakes
preparation: Mix oat flour, baking powder in a bowl.
Whisk egg, milk, honey, vanilla. Add to dry ingredients.
Let batter rest 5 minutes.
Cook on buttered pan until bubbles form, flip.
Serve with maple syrup and berries.
notes: Oat flour makes these gluten-friendlier. Don't overmix.

name: Buckwheat Pancakes
preparation: Mix buckwheat flour, sugar, baking soda, and salt.
Whisk egg, buttermilk, and melted butter. Add to dry mix.
Let rest 5 minutes.
Cook on medium heat until golden on both sides.
Serve with honey and fresh fruit.
notes: Buckwheat has a nutty flavor. Naturally gluten-free.

name: Protein Pancakes
preparation: Blend all ingredients in a blender until smooth.
Heat pan with cooking spray on medium heat.
Pour small rounds of batter.
Cook 2-3 minutes per side.
Serve with Greek yogurt and berries.
notes: High protein breakfast for fitness enthusiasts.

name: Scrambled Eggs
preparation: Whisk eggs with milk, salt, and pepper.
Melt butter in a non-stick pan on low heat.
Pour egg mixture. Stir gently with a spatula.
Cook on low heat, folding gently until just set but still creamy.
Garnish with herbs and serve immediately.
notes: Low heat and gentle stirring are key for creamy scrambled eggs.

name: Poached Eggs On Toast
preparation: Bring water to a gentle simmer. Add vinegar.
Create a swirl in the water. Crack egg into the center.
Cook for 3-4 minutes for runny yolk.
Remove with slotted spoon.
Place on buttered toast. Season with salt and pepper.
notes: Fresh eggs poach better. Water should simmer, not boil.

name: Egg White Omelette
preparation: Whisk egg whites with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in non-stick pan.
Add vegetables and saute 2 minutes.
Pour egg whites over vegetables.
Cook on low heat until set. Fold in half.
Serve immediately.
notes: Low-fat, high-protein option. Great for weight management.

name: Veggie Omelette
preparation: Whisk eggs with salt and pepper.
Melt butter in pan. Add all vegetables, saute 2 minutes.
Pour eggs over vegetables. Cook on low heat.
When almost set, add cheese on one half.
Fold omelette over. Cook 1 more minute.
Serve with toast.
notes: Cook on low heat for a tender, fluffy omelette.

name: Smoothie Bowl Tropical
preparation: Blend frozen banana, mango, and coconut milk until thick and smooth.
Pour into a bowl.
Top with granola, coconut, kiwi, and chia seeds.
Serve immediately.
notes: Use frozen fruit for thick consistency. Don't add too much liquid.

name: Acai Bowl
ingredients: Toppings: granola, banana slices, berries, coconut flakes, honey
preparation: Blend acai puree, frozen banana, berries, and almond milk until thick.
Pour into a bowl.
Top with granola, fresh banana, berries, coconut.
Drizzle honey and serve immediately.
notes: Should be thick like soft serve. Add liquid sparingly.

name: Dragon Fruit Bowl
ingredients: Toppings: granola, kiwi, berries, coconut flakes, seeds
preparation: Blend dragon fruit, banana, and coconut milk until thick.
Pour into a bowl.
Arrange toppings attractively.
Serve immediately.
notes: Beautiful pink color. Use frozen dragon fruit packets.

name: Mango Smoothie Bowl
ingredients: Toppings: granola, mango cubes, coconut, chia seeds
preparation: Blend frozen mango, banana, yogurt, and milk until thick.
Pour into a bowl.
Top with granola, fresh mango, coconut, and chia seeds.
Serve immediately.
notes: Use Indian alphonso mango for the best flavor.

name: Whole Wheat French Toast
ingredients: Maple syrup and fruit for serving
preparation: Whisk eggs, milk, cinnamon, and vanilla.
Dip bread slices in egg mixture, coating both sides.
Melt butter in a pan on medium heat.
Cook dipped bread 2-3 minutes each side until golden.
Serve with maple syrup and fresh fruit.
notes: Whole wheat adds fiber. Don't soak bread too long.

name: Cinnamon Raisin Toast
preparation: Toast bread until golden.
Spread cream cheese or butter.
Drizzle honey and sprinkle cinnamon.
Serve immediately.
notes: Simple, sweet, and satisfying.

name: Peanut Butter Toast
preparation: Toast bread until golden.
Spread peanut butter evenly.
Top with banana slices.
Drizzle honey and sprinkle chia seeds.
notes: Good source of protein and healthy fats.

name: Chia Seed Pudding
preparation: Mix chia seeds, milk, honey, and vanilla in a jar.
Stir well. Refrigerate overnight (at least 4 hours).
Stir again before serving.
Top with fresh fruit and nuts.
Serve cold.
notes: Chia absorbs liquid and creates a pudding-like texture. Must soak.

name: Flax Seed Porridge
preparation: Heat milk in a pan.
Add ground flax seeds, stirring constantly.
Cook 2-3 minutes until thick.
Add honey and cinnamon.
Top with banana and walnuts.
notes: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids. Use ground flax for absorption.

name: Quinoa Breakfast Bowl
preparation: Warm cooked quinoa with milk.
Add honey and cinnamon. Stir well.
Top with banana, almonds, and berries.
Serve warm.
notes: Quinoa is a complete protein. Great alternative to oatmeal.

name: Turkey Bacon Wrap
preparation: Cook turkey bacon until crispy.
Scramble eggs with salt and pepper.
Warm tortillas.
Layer eggs, turkey bacon, cheese, avocado, and salsa.
Roll tightly into wraps. Serve.
notes: High-protein wrap. Turkey bacon is leaner than regular bacon.

name: Smoked Salmon Bagel Light
preparation: Toast bagel halves.
Spread cream cheese on each half.
Layer smoked salmon on top.
Add capers, onion slices, and dill.
Squeeze lemon over and serve.
notes: Use light cream cheese for fewer calories. Rich in omega-3.

name: Cottage Cheese Bowl
preparation: Place cottage cheese in a bowl.
Top with pineapple, walnuts, and flax seeds.
Drizzle honey.
Serve cold.
notes: High protein and low effort. Great for quick mornings.

name: Muesli Bowl
preparation: Pour muesli in bowl.
Add milk or yogurt.
Top with banana and seeds.
Drizzle honey. Serve.
notes: Soak muesli overnight in milk for a softer texture.

name: Bircher Muesli
preparation: Mix oats, milk, yogurt, grated apple, honey, and lemon juice.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Top with nuts before serving.
Serve cold.
notes: Swiss-style overnight muesli. Grated apple adds freshness.

name: Swiss Muesli
ingredients: 2 tbsp mixed nuts and dried fruits
preparation: Soak oats in milk overnight in fridge.
Add cream, grated apple, lemon juice, and honey.
Mix well.
Top with nuts and dried fruits.
Serve cold.
notes: Traditional Swiss recipe by Dr. Bircher-Benner.

name: Egg Muffins
preparation: Preheat oven to 180°C. Grease muffin tin.
Whisk eggs with salt and pepper.
Divide vegetables and cheese among muffin cups.
Pour egg mixture into each cup.
Bake 20-25 minutes until set.
Cool slightly and serve.
