notes: Meal prep friendly - make ahead and reheat.

name: Spinach Egg Cups
preparation: Preheat oven to 180°C. Grease ramekins.
Line with spinach leaves.
Place 1 tbsp cream cheese in each.
Crack an egg into each ramekin.
Season with salt and pepper.
Bake 15-18 minutes until eggs are set.
notes: Watch the baking time - yolk should be slightly runny.

name: Sweet Potato Hash
preparation: Heat oil in a skillet. Add sweet potato.
Cook 8-10 minutes until tender.
Add bell pepper and onion. Cook 5 more minutes.
Season with paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Optionally top with fried eggs.
Garnish with herbs and serve.
notes: Dice sweet potato small for faster cooking.

name: Shakshuka Light
preparation: Heat oil, saute onion and pepper until soft.
Add garlic, cumin, paprika, chilli flakes. Cook 1 minute.
Add crushed tomatoes and salt. Simmer 10 minutes.
Make wells and crack eggs into sauce.
Cover and cook 5-8 minutes until eggs are set.
Garnish with parsley. Serve with bread.
notes: Middle Eastern classic. Eggs should have runny yolks.

name: Huevos Rancheros Light
preparation: Warm tortillas on a dry pan.
Fry eggs in oil (sunny side up).
Warm black beans with a pinch of cumin.
Place tortillas on plate, top with beans and egg.
Add salsa and avocado.
Garnish with coriander and lime.
notes: Mexican breakfast classic. Use corn tortillas for authenticity.

name: Breakfast Burrito Light
preparation: Scramble eggs with salt and pepper.
Warm tortilla.
Layer beans, scrambled eggs, cheese, salsa, and avocado.
Add hot sauce if desired.
Roll tightly into a burrito.
Toast seam-side down on pan if desired.
notes: Can be made ahead and frozen for meal prep.

name: Fruit Salad
preparation: Cut all fruits into bite-sized pieces.
Combine in a bowl.
Squeeze lime juice, drizzle honey.
Sprinkle chaat masala for Indian twist.
Toss gently and serve fresh.
notes: Use seasonal fruits. Chaat masala adds a delightful twist.

name: Watermelon Breakfast Bowl
preparation: Arrange watermelon cubes in a bowl.
Add dollop of yogurt.
Sprinkle granola and pumpkin seeds.
Drizzle honey and add mint.
Serve fresh and cold.
notes: Refreshing summer breakfast. Very hydrating.

name: Cantaloupe Prosciutto Light
preparation: Arrange cantaloupe wedges on a plate.
Drape prosciutto slices over melon.
Garnish with mint and black pepper.
Drizzle balsamic glaze lightly.
Serve immediately.
notes: Classic Italian combination. Sweet and savory.

name: Almond Butter Banana Wrap
preparation: Warm tortilla slightly.
Spread almond butter evenly.
Place whole banana on one side.
Drizzle honey and sprinkle chia seeds and cinnamon.
Roll tightly. Slice in half and serve.
notes: Quick grab-and-go breakfast. Great pre-workout option.

name: Rice Cake With Nut Butter
preparation: Spread nut butter on rice cakes.
Top with banana slices.
Drizzle honey and sprinkle hemp seeds.
Serve immediately.
notes: Light, crunchy, and satisfying. Gluten-free option.

name: Tahini Toast
preparation: Toast bread until golden.
Spread tahini evenly.
Top with banana slices.
Drizzle honey, sprinkle sesame seeds and sea salt.
Serve immediately.
notes: Tahini adds calcium and a unique nutty flavor.

name: Baked Oatmeal Cups
preparation: Preheat oven to 180°C. Grease muffin tin.
Mix all ingredients together.
Divide into muffin cups.
Bake 20-25 minutes until set and golden.
Cool slightly and serve.
notes: Great meal prep option. Stores well for 4-5 days.

name: Apple Pie Oatmeal
preparation: Saute diced apple in butter with cinnamon and nutmeg for 3 minutes.
Add milk and bring to simmer.
Add oats and cook 3-5 minutes.
Add brown sugar.
Top with pecans and serve warm.
notes: Tastes like apple pie for breakfast.

name: Pumpkin Spice Oats
preparation: Cook oats with milk for 3-5 minutes.
Stir in pumpkin puree, maple syrup, and spice.
Cook 1 more minute.
Top with pecans and whipped cream if desired.
Serve warm.
notes: Autumn-inspired breakfast. Use canned pumpkin puree.

name: Tofu Scramble
preparation: Press tofu to remove water. Crumble with hands.
Heat oil, saute onion and pepper 3 minutes.
Add crumbled tofu, turmeric, cumin, salt, pepper.
Cook 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add spinach and nutritional yeast. Cook 1 more minute.
Serve with toast.
notes: Vegan alternative to scrambled eggs. Turmeric gives egg-like color.

name: Vegan Breakfast Hash
preparation: Heat oil in skillet. Add potatoes and sweet potato.
Cook 10-12 minutes until tender.
Add bell pepper and onion. Cook 5 minutes.
Add black beans, paprika, cumin, salt, pepper.
Cook 2-3 more minutes.
Serve with sliced avocado.
notes: Hearty vegan breakfast. Can add any vegetables you like.

name: Tempeh Bacon Bowl
preparation: Marinate tempeh in soy sauce, maple syrup, and paprika for 10 minutes.
Pan fry tempeh in oil until crispy on both sides.
Arrange quinoa in bowl, top with spinach.
Add crispy tempeh strips.
Add sliced avocado and serve.
notes: Tempeh bacon is a protein-rich vegan alternative.

name: Skyr With Berries
preparation: Place skyr in a bowl.
Top with mixed berries.
Add granola and flax seeds.
Drizzle honey.
Serve cold.
notes: Skyr is very high in protein and naturally thick.

name: Kefir Smoothie
preparation: Add all ingredients to a blender.
Blend until smooth.
Pour into a glass.
Serve immediately.
notes: Kefir is rich in probiotics. Great for gut health.

name: Cottage Cheese Pancakes
preparation: Blend cottage cheese, eggs, oat flour, honey, and vanilla.
Heat butter in pan.
Pour small rounds of batter.
Cook 2-3 minutes each side until golden.
Serve with fruit and maple syrup.
notes: High protein pancakes. Cottage cheese makes them fluffy.

name: Cornmeal Porridge
preparation: Mix cornmeal with water to make a paste.
Bring milk to a boil.
Add cornmeal paste slowly, stirring constantly.
Cook on low heat 10-15 minutes, stirring.
Add sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, butter, and salt.
Serve warm.
notes: Caribbean-style porridge. Stir constantly to prevent lumps.

name: Grits Bowl Light
preparation: Bring water and milk to a boil.
Add grits slowly, stirring.
Reduce heat and cook 20-25 minutes, stirring often.
Add butter, cheese, salt, and pepper.
Serve hot.
notes: Southern US staple. Slow cooking yields the creamiest grits.

name: Polenta
preparation: Bring water and milk to a boil.
Add polenta slowly, stirring.
Cook on low 15-20 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add butter, parmesan, salt, and pepper.
Serve with a fried egg on top if desired.
notes: Italian-style creamy polenta. Versatile base for any topping.

name: Waffles Whole Wheat
preparation: Mix flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt.
Whisk eggs, milk, butter, and vanilla separately.
Combine wet and dry ingredients. Don't overmix.
Preheat waffle iron. Pour batter and cook until golden.
Serve with maple syrup and fresh berries.
notes: Don't overmix batter - small lumps are fine.

name: Crepes Light
preparation: Whisk all ingredients into a smooth, thin batter.
Rest batter 30 minutes.
Heat a crepe pan with a little butter.
Pour thin layer of batter, swirl to coat.
Cook 1-2 minutes, flip carefully, cook 30 seconds.
Fill with Nutella, fruit, or savory fillings.
notes: Batter must rest for tender crepes. Swirl quickly.

name: Dutch Baby Pancake Light
ingredients: Lemon and powdered sugar for serving
preparation: Preheat oven to 220°C with a cast iron skillet inside.
Blend eggs, flour, milk, sugar, vanilla, and salt.
Add butter to hot skillet, swirl to coat.
Pour batter immediately.
Bake 15-20 minutes until puffed and golden.
Serve with lemon juice and powdered sugar.
notes: Will puff dramatically in oven and deflate. That's normal!

name: Breakfast Quinoa With Almond Milk
preparation: Cook quinoa with almond milk on low heat for 15 minutes.
Add maple syrup and cinnamon.
Top with almond butter, berries, and almonds.
Serve warm.
notes: Vegan breakfast bowl. Almond milk gives a nutty flavor.

name: Amaranth Porridge
preparation: Bring water to a boil. Add amaranth.
Reduce heat and simmer 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add milk, honey, and cinnamon.
Cook 5 more minutes.
Top with pumpkin seeds and fruit.
notes: Amaranth is an ancient grain rich in protein and iron.

name: Teff Porridge
preparation: Bring water to a boil. Add teff.
Simmer 15-20 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add milk and cook 5 more minutes.
Add honey and cinnamon.
Top with walnuts and banana.
notes: Ethiopian grain. Naturally gluten-free and nutritious.

name: Smoked Turkey Breakfast Wrap
preparation: Scramble egg. Warm tortilla.
Spread mustard on tortilla.
Layer turkey, scrambled egg, avocado, and arugula.
Season with salt and pepper.
Roll tightly and serve.
notes: Quick, protein-rich breakfast wrap.

name: Veggie Breakfast Sandwich
preparation: Toast English muffin.
Fry egg to desired doneness.
Spread mashed avocado on bottom half.
Layer spinach, tomato, cheese, and fried egg.
Season and close with top half. Serve.
notes: Customize with your favorite vegetables.

name: Matcha Overnight Oats
preparation: Mix oats, milk, yogurt, matcha, honey, and chia seeds.
Stir well until matcha is evenly distributed.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Top with fresh fruit before serving.
notes: Matcha adds caffeine and antioxidants. Sift matcha to avoid lumps.

name: Turmeric Oats
preparation: Heat coconut oil, add turmeric and other spices. Bloom for 30 seconds.
Add milk and bring to a simmer.
Add oats and cook 3-5 minutes.
Add honey and black pepper.
Serve warm with fruit if desired.
notes: Anti-inflammatory golden oats. Black pepper enhances turmeric absorption.

name: Cacao Oats
preparation: Cook oats with milk for 3-5 minutes.
Stir in cacao powder and honey.
Top with banana, peanut butter, and cacao nibs.
Serve warm.
notes: Chocolate for breakfast! Cacao is healthier than cocoa.

name: Egg Fried Rice Morning Style
preparation: Heat oil on high. Scramble eggs, set aside.
Add onion, peas, and corn. Stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add rice and stir-fry on high heat 3-4 minutes.
Add soy sauce, sesame oil, eggs back.
Garnish with green onion. Serve hot.
notes: Use cold day-old rice for non-sticky fried rice.

name: Japanese Breakfast Rice
preparation: Warm rice. Place in bowl.
Crack a raw egg over hot rice (tamago kake gohan).
Add soy sauce and mix.
Top with nori and furikake.
Serve with miso soup and pickled vegetables on side.
notes: Traditional Japanese breakfast. Use very fresh eggs.

name: Mediterranean Breakfast Plate
preparation: Boil or fry eggs.
Arrange hummus, cucumber, tomato, olives, and feta on plate.
Warm pita bread.
Add eggs and garnish with herbs.
Serve with olive oil drizzle.
notes: Balanced and colorful. Classic Mediterranean flavors.

name: Turkish Menemen Light
preparation: Heat oil. Saute onion and pepper until soft.
Add tomatoes and cook until they break down (5-7 minutes).
Add red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper.
Pour eggs over the vegetable mixture.
Stir gently until eggs are just set.
Garnish with parsley. Serve with bread.
notes: Turkish-style scrambled eggs with tomatoes and peppers.

name: Banana Bread Oatmeal
preparation: Cook oats with milk for 3-5 minutes.
Add mashed banana, brown sugar, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
Stir well and cook 1 more minute.
Top with walnuts and a pat of butter.
Serve warm.
notes: Tastes like banana bread in a bowl. Use very ripe banana.

name: Air Fryer Aloo Tikki
preparation: Mash potatoes, mix in peas and spices.
Add cornflour and shape into round patties.
Spray air fryer basket with oil.
Place tikkis in basket, spray tops lightly.
Air fry at 180°C for 12-14 minutes, flipping halfway.
Serve hot with green chutney.
notes: Much less oil than traditional shallow-fried version.

name: Air Fryer Bread Pakora
preparation: Mix besan, water, ajwain, chilli powder and salt into a thick batter.
Cut each bread slice diagonally.
Dip bread triangles in batter, coat evenly.
Place in air fryer basket, spray lightly with oil.
Air fry at 190°C for 8-10 minutes, flipping once.
Serve with mint chutney.
notes: Crispy outside, soft inside with minimal oil.

name: Air Fryer Paneer Tikka Wrap
preparation: Marinate paneer cubes in curd and tikka masala for 15 min.
Thread paneer, capsicum and onion on skewers or place in basket.
Air fry at 200°C for 10 minutes.
Warm roti, fill with air-fried paneer and veggies.
Roll tightly and serve.
notes: High-protein breakfast wrap.

name: Air Fryer Moong Dal Chilla
preparation: Grind soaked moong dal with chilli and ginger into smooth batter.
Mix in onion and coriander.
Pour thin layer in air fryer-safe pan.
Air fry at 180°C for 8 minutes until set and golden.
Flip and cook 3 more minutes.
Serve with green chutney.
notes: Protein-rich, gluten-free breakfast.

name: Air Fryer Egg Muffins
preparation: Whisk eggs with milk, salt and pepper.
Add diced vegetables and spinach.
Pour into silicone muffin moulds.
Place in air fryer at 170°C for 12 minutes.
Let cool for 2 minutes before removing.
Serve warm.
notes: Meal-prep friendly, keeps 3 days in fridge.

name: Air Fryer Besan Chilla
preparation: Mix besan with water to a smooth batter.
Add onion, chilli, coriander and turmeric.
Pour into greased air fryer pan.
Air fry at 180°C for 10 minutes.
Flip and cook 3 more minutes.
Serve with tomato ketchup or chutney.
notes: Classic besan chilla with almost no oil.

name: Air Fryer Poha Cutlet
preparation: Drain poha well and mix with mashed potato.
Add crushed peanuts, chilli, coriander and salt.
Shape into round cutlets.
Spray with oil and place in air fryer.
Air fry at 180°C for 14 minutes, flipping once.
Serve hot with chutney.
notes: A creative twist on classic poha.

name: Air Fryer Masala Omelette
preparation: Whisk eggs with all ingredients.
Pour into greased air fryer-safe dish.
Air fry at 170°C for 8-10 minutes.
No flipping needed.
Slide onto plate and serve with toast.
notes: Hands-free omelette, evenly cooked.

name: Air Fryer Samosa
preparation: Heat a small pan, add cumin seeds.
Add mashed potato, peas and all spices. Mix well.
Fill samosa sheets with potato mixture and seal edges.
Spray air fryer basket with oil, place samosas.
Spray tops lightly.
Air fry at 180°C for 15 minutes until golden and crispy.
Serve with tamarind or green chutney.
notes: 90% less oil than deep-fried samosas.

name: Air Fryer Spinach Corn Sandwich
preparation: Mix spinach, corn, cream cheese, pepper and salt.
Spread filling on one bread slice, cover with other.
Spray both sides with oil.
Air fry at 180°C for 6-8 minutes until golden.
Cut diagonally and serve.
notes: Crispy, cheesy and nutritious.

name: Air Fryer Vegetable Cutlet
ingredients: 1/2 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, beans, peas), boiled and mashed
preparation: Mix mashed vegetables and potato with spices and cornflour.
Shape into oval cutlets.
Roll in breadcrumbs.
Spray with oil and place in air fryer.
Air fry at 180°C for 12-14 minutes, flipping once.
Serve with ketchup.
notes: Crispy coating with soft veggie filling.

name: Air Fryer Methi Paratha
preparation: Mix flour, methi, ajwain, chilli powder and salt.
Knead into soft dough with water.
Roll into thin parathas.
Spray air fryer basket, place paratha.
Air fry at 180°C for 4 minutes per side.
Spray lightly when flipping.
Serve with curd or pickle.
notes: Crispy methi paratha without the tawa oil.

name: Air Fryer Cheese Toast
preparation: Spread butter on bread slices.
Top with grated cheese, oregano and chilli flakes.
Place in air fryer butter-side down.
Air fry at 180°C for 5-6 minutes until cheese melts.
Serve immediately.
notes: Quick cheesy breakfast in minutes.

name: Air Fryer Dahi Vada Bites
preparation: Grind dal into smooth batter.
Mix in cumin seeds and salt.
Drop small spoonfuls into greased air fryer mould.
Air fry at 180°C for 12 minutes.
Soak vadas in warm water for 10 min, squeeze gently.
Top with curd, cumin powder and tamarind chutney.
notes: Light dahi vadas without deep frying.

name: Air Fryer Banana Chips
preparation: Soak banana slices in turmeric water for 5 min.
Drain and pat dry.
Spray with oil and arrange in single layer.
Air fry at 180°C for 12-15 minutes, shaking basket every 5 min.
Season with salt while hot.
Cool completely for maximum crunch.
notes: Kerala-style banana chips, fraction of the oil.

name: Air Fryer Mushroom Toast
preparation: Toss mushrooms with garlic, onion, pepper and salt.
Air fry mushroom mixture at 180°C for 8 minutes.
Toast bread in air fryer for 2 minutes.
Top toast with air-fried mushrooms.
Drizzle with melted butter.
notes: Earthy, savoury breakfast toast.

name: Air Fryer Sweet Potato Hash
preparation: Toss sweet potato with oil and all seasonings.
Air fry at 190°C for 15 minutes, shaking halfway.
Add onion and capsicum, air fry 5 more minutes.
Serve as a side or with eggs.
notes: Naturally sweet and filling breakfast.

name: Air Fryer Paneer Paratha
preparation: Mix flour with salt and knead into dough.
Combine paneer, cumin, chilli and coriander for stuffing.
Roll dough, stuff with paneer mix, re-roll.
Spray and place in air fryer.
Air fry at 180°C for 4-5 minutes per side.
Serve with raita.
notes: Protein-rich stuffed paratha.

name: Air Fryer French Toast
preparation: Whisk egg, milk, cinnamon and honey.
Dip bread slices in egg mixture.
Spray air fryer basket, place bread.
Air fry at 170°C for 5 minutes per side.
Serve with fresh fruit.
notes: Golden and custardy with no pan-frying.

name: Air Fryer Upma Balls
preparation: Dry roast rava until light golden.
In a pan, heat a little oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion, chilli, peanuts and sauté.
Add water and salt, bring to boil.
Add rava, stir well, cover and cook 3 min.
Cool, shape into balls.
Air fry at 180°C for 10-12 minutes until crispy.
notes: A fun twist on classic upma.

name: Grilled Paneer Sandwich
preparation: Spread green chutney on bread.
Layer paneer, capsicum and onion.
Butter outside of bread.
Grill on hot tawa or sandwich press until golden.
Cut and serve.
notes: Classic Indian grilled sandwich.

name: Grilled Egg Wrap
preparation: Scramble eggs with onion, tomato, pepper and salt.
Place in warm tortilla.
Roll and grill on tawa for 2 min per side.
Serve with ketchup.
notes: Protein-rich morning wrap.

name: Grilled Vegetable Toast
preparation: Top bread with veggies and cheese.
Sprinkle oregano.
Grill on tawa or press until cheese melts.
Serve hot.
notes: Cheesy veggie toast.

name: Grilled Corn Toast
preparation: Mix corn with cheese and chilli flakes.
Spread on bread, butter outside.
Grill until golden.
Serve warm.
notes: Sweet and spicy grilled toast.

name: Grilled Banana Peanut Butter Toast
preparation: Spread peanut butter on bread.
Layer banana slices.
Drizzle honey and cinnamon.
Grill on tawa for 2 min per side.
notes: Sweet, filling breakfast.

name: Grilled Masala Omelette Sandwich
preparation: Beat eggs with onion, chilli, coriander, turmeric and salt.
Cook thin omelette on tawa.
Place between bread slices.
Grill until bread is golden.
notes: Hearty egg sandwich.

name: Grilled Aloo Sandwich
preparation: Mix potato with spices.
Spread chutney on bread, fill with potato mix.
Butter outside and grill until golden.
notes: Bombay-style grilled sandwich.

name: Grilled Mushroom on Toast
preparation: Sauté mushrooms with garlic, thyme, salt and pepper in butter.
Pile onto toasted bread.
Grill briefly to warm.
notes: Earthy garlicky mushroom toast.

name: Grilled Chutney Cheese Sandwich
preparation: Spread chutney on one slice, layer cheese, onion, tomato.
Close sandwich, butter outside.
Grill until cheese melts and bread is golden.
notes: Mumbai street-style grilled sandwich.

name: Grilled Sweet Potato Toast
preparation: Grill sweet potato slices on tawa or grill for 5 min per side until soft.
Spread mashed avocado on top.
Squeeze lemon, add chilli flakes and salt.
notes: Grain-free toast alternative.
